assertions.text: [rubric-1] Implements correct FizzBuzz logic for multiples of 3, 5, and 15
assertions.evidence: The code checks divisibility in the correct order (15 before 3 and 5), so multiples of 15 print "fizzbuzz", multiples of 3 print "fizz", and multiples of 5 print "buzz".

assertions.text: Generated code is executable FizzBuzz-style Python
assertions.evidence: Found loop, print call, and 3/5 fizzbuzz branch markers.

assertions.text: [rubric-1] Solution is simple and idiomatic Python
assertions.evidence: The code is concise and idiomatic: a small function, standard range loop, straightforward modulo checks, and clear branch ordering. It is simple Python style.
It handles 15 first, then 3, then 5, printing "fizzbuzz", "fizz", and "buzz" in lowercase respectively; this correctly prioritizes the 15-case over separate 3/5 cases.
